hawkish_phrases: Inflation was still expected to stay too high for too long, and domestic price pressures remained strong
domestic inflation remained persistent and had hovered around 5.5%
the Governing Council should continue to stress its determination to set policy rates...at sufficiently restrictive levels for as long as necessary
the Governing Council should be ready...for further interest rate hikes if necessary
it was cautioned against declaring victory over inflation at the current stage, when inflation was still more than twice the ECB's target
dovish_phrases: inflation had dropped markedly in September to 4.3%
most measures of underlying inflation had continued to ease
The economy was likely to remain weak for the remainder of 2023
nowcasting tools indicated lower growth in the third and fourth quarters of 2023...and even implied that there would be a technical recession
weaker demand...would ease price pressures, especially over the medium term
summary: The Governing Council unanimously held all three key ECB interest rates at current levels after ten consecutive hikes, maintaining a restrictive stance with emphasis that rates would remain sufficiently restrictive for as long as necessary while keeping the door open for further hikes if needed, despite inflation falling to 4.3% and signs of economic weakness.
